authors_affiliation: Hardik Rajpal, Madalina Sas, Chris Lockwood, Rebecca Joakim, Nicholas S Peters, Max Falkenberg; Centre for Complexity Science, Imperial College London (UK), with contributors from Wexham Park Hospital and an independent researcher (Luxembourg)

result.evaluation_split: official hidden test set (out of 41 successful entries); validation leaderboard score was 0.533 prior to final test scoring

self_reported_metrics.other: On training/validation data (with IT features, threshold 0.9): AUPRC 0.55, Precision 0.776, F2 score 0.626 (improved to 0.739 after threshold tuning). Without IT features: Accuracy 0.379, Precision 0.804, Recall 0.934, F2 0.601 (0.731 after threshold tuning).

model.architecture_details: Per-condition binary classifiers using XGBoost (gradient-boosted decision tree ensembles), one ensemble trained independently per scored heart condition, with a tuned probability threshold per class for binarization.
model.key_novelty: Combines standard ECG waveform/interval/power-spectrum features with information-theoretic measures borrowed from neuroscience (normalized Lempel-Ziv complexity of the binarized signal, and synergy/redundancy captured via O-Information and S-Information across leads) as inputs to an interpretable per-condition XGBoost classifier.

input.preprocessing: Second-order Butterworth band-pass filter (1.5-25 Hz) per channel, followed by 'db4' discrete wavelet transform denoising; segment/interval features normalized by average RR interval, wave heights normalized by absolute R peak height.

data.training_datasets: Combined official Challenge training datasets acquired from four different countries (specific per-database breakdown not given)
data.class_imbalance_handling: Random undersampling of the majority class to match the minority class population, performed separately for each binary per-condition classifier

training.loss_function: Precision-Recall Area Under the Curve (AUPRC) minimized during training; F2 metric used for early stopping
training.optimizer: not reported (XGBoost gradient boosting; hyperparameters selected via Grid Search: max_depth=10, eta=0.1, gamma=0, lambda=1, with early stopping)
training.cross_validation_strategy: K-fold cross-validation used both for parameter tuning/robustness checks and for threshold optimization

contribution.summary: Proposes an interpretable per-condition XGBoost classifier for 12-lead ECG classification that augments standard ECG waveform features with information-theoretic measures (Lempel-Ziv complexity and O-Information-based synergy/redundancy across leads) adapted from neuroscience.
